reasoning: The Lean statement accurately captures the informal claim. The hypothesis `card G = 56` with `[Group G] [Fintype G]` matches "a group of order 56". The conclusion asserts existence of a prime p dividing |G| and a Sylow p-subgroup P that is normal, which precisely matches "has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order". The `Sylow p G` type in Mathlib corresponds to maximal p-subgroups, which coincides with the standard mathematical definition when p is prime and divides |G|. The quantifier structure (∃ p, ∃ P, conditions) aligns with the informal "for some prime".
